cases.title: Testing-plan Session lifecycle and QR row: projection display receives rotating token metadata while Open
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Class session is in Open state per testing-plan §3.1 Session lifecycle and QR coverage block.
PG-05 QrDisplayPanel data source is wired to GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and capture qrPayload, expiresAt, and tokenState.
Assert expiresAt minus meta.timestamp is approximately 30 seconds and tokenState is Valid.
Wait until after expiresAt and call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current again without closing the session.
Compare qrPayload and expiresAt between the two responses.
Verify session state remains Open and display metadata remains projection-ready across rotation.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan Session lifecycle and QR row: AC-02 Given/When/Then satisfied for display layer — each token valid 30 seconds, display refreshes to new valid token without lecturer reload, suitable for classroom projection per FR-14.

cases.title: M04 QR Orchestrator issues projection-ready token payload bound to session with session metadata for display
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for qr_session_tokens and class_sessions tables.
An Open class session row exists with known sessionId, section code, and session name.
M04 issueToken(sessionId) and session read path are callable through service layer.
steps: Invoke M04 getCurrentToken(sessionId) for the Open session.
Query qr_session_tokens and assert Valid row bound to sessionId with qrPayload resolvable server-side.
Join class_sessions and class_sections to retrieve section code and session display name.
Assert returned display bundle includes classSessionId, tokenState Valid, expiresAt, qrPayload, and session state Open.
Advance harness clock beyond TTL and re-invoke getCurrentToken; assert refreshed qrPayload with new 30-second window.
cases.expected: M04 module + repository integration returns projection-ready payload per WF-02 §4.3 checkpoint: token metadata and session identity fields required by QrDisplayPanel are coherently bound without trusting client-supplied session hints.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current returns 200 projection-ready envelope for Open session
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with Authorization bearer token.
Target session is currently Open with known section code and session name.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current with Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error} with error null.
Assert data contains classSessionId matching path param, tokenState Valid, expiresAt ISO-8601 UTC, and non-empty qrPayload suitable for QR canvas rendering.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp for countdown synchronization.
Re-call endpoint after one TTL rollover and confirm HTTP 200 with refreshed expiresAt and updated qrPayload.
cases.expected: Endpoint returns HTTP 200 with stable envelope schema per API design §4.4; qr/current data exposes all fields required to render large high-contrast QR with countdown indicator on lecturer projection view.

cases.title: Lecturer authenticates via login then fetches projection QR metadata for assigned Open session
preconditions: Lecturer credentials exist for POST /v1/auth/login.
An Open session exists in a section assigned to that Lecturer.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with Lecturer credentials and capture accessToken from HTTP 200 envelope.
Send GET /v1/me with bearer token and assert Lecturer identity and assigned section scope.
Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current with bearer token.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.tokenState Valid, data.qrPayload present, data.expiresAt within 30-second window, and data.classSessionId matches the assigned Open session.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken; GET /v1/me confirms Lecturer scope; subsequent qr/current succeeds with projection-ready Valid token metadata for the assigned Open session only.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance provides live counts alongside Open QR display context
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Open session with enrollments exists and QR tokens are actively rotating on PG-05.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and confirm tokenState Valid with qrPayload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization.
Validate HTTP 200 and envelope shape.
Assert data.state is Open, counts object includes present/late/pending/rejectedAttempts keys matching wireframe PG-05 summary strip.
Confirm attendance state Open aligns with QR display Open status badge requirement.
cases.expected: Attendance roster endpoint returns HTTP 200 with data.state Open and live count payload while FR-14 QR display remains active; PG-05 can render session status badge and summary counts consistent with backend state.

cases.title: Primary lifecycle flow: open session enables projection QR display with session identity and rotation
preconditions: Lecturer account and Scheduled session fixture are available.
Session has section code, session name, and no prior openedAt timestamp.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Open with qr preview containing expiresAt and qrPayload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and verify tokenState Valid bound to the same sessionId.
Fetch session list GET /v1/class-sessions?classSectionId={sectionId} and locate opened session with section code and Open state.
Wait one TTL cycle and confirm qr/current returns refreshed token without session reopen.
cases.expected: WF-01 / FLOW-05 happy path: session open commits Open state and projection display receives first Valid QR with session name, section code, countdown metadata, and auto-rotation every ~30 seconds without lecturer reload.

cases.title: Alternate flow: session close transitions projection display from live QR to locked closed state
preconditions: Open session with active QR rotation is displayed on lecturer view.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Enrolled students exist with mixed attendance states.
steps: Confirm GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current returns tokenState Valid.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Closed with closedAt timestamp.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and record whether tokenState is non-Valid or endpoint returns appropriate error.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and assert data.state is Closed.
cases.expected: After Open to Closed transition, projection display must not show active rotating QR; closed state message replaces QR panel per QrDisplayPanel states spec; attendance endpoint confirms Closed status badge.

cases.title: QrDisplayPanel data binding follows SM-01 session state: Open enables QR, Closed and Cancelled suppress Valid token
preconditions: Session fixtures exist in Scheduled, Open, Closed, and Cancelled states.
M03 session control and M04 QR orchestrator share the same database.
Display read path queries session state before returning QR metadata.
steps: Attempt qr/current read for Scheduled session and record absence of Valid token or appropriate guard response.
Execute open transition (Scheduled to Open) and verify Valid token with expiresAt is returned.
Record beforeState Open with active qrPayload; invoke close transition (Open to Closed).
Query qr/current for Closed session and assert no Valid token with active qrPayload.
Attempt qr/current on Cancelled session fixture and assert QR display data is suppressed.
cases.expected: beforeState Scheduled blocks Valid QR display data; Open enables projection-ready Valid token; after Open to Closed or Cancelled, display binding suppresses active QR per QrDisplayPanel Closed/Cancelled states.

cases.title: Student, ITAdmin, and out-of-scope Lecturer are denied QR projection display endpoint
preconditions: Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer-not-assigned-to-section test accounts exist with valid tokens.
An Open session exists in a section the out-of-scope Lecturer is not assigned to.
Assigned Lecturer token is available as positive control.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as Student.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as ITAdmin.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each denied request (expect 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope).
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as assigned Lecturer and assert HTTP 200 with Valid token metadata.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as AcademicAdmin and assert override access per API design §4.4 roles.
cases.expected: Unauthorized roles cannot retrieve QR projection metadata; assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin may fetch current QR per SessionControl permission matrix; no qrPayload leakage to denied actors.

cases.title: QR display endpoint rejects or omits Valid token when session is not Open
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Session fixtures exist in Scheduled and Closed states.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/qr/current for a Scheduled session.
Record HTTP status and whether data.tokenState is absent or non-Valid.
Open a separate session, then close it; call GET /v1/class-sessions/{closedSessionId}/qr/current.
Assert response does not return tokenState Valid with active qrPayload for Closed session.
Verify projection UI would render locked/hidden state rather than scannable QR for non-Open sessions.
cases.expected: FR-14 restricts active projection QR to Open sessions only: Scheduled and Closed targets do not expose active Valid rotating QR metadata; display shows appropriate non-scannable locked state.

cases.title: PG-05 QrDisplayPanel renders large high-contrast QR with section code, session name, countdown, and Open badge
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
An Open session is available on PG-05 session control page.
QrDisplayPanel (DC-01) and QrCountdownRing (DC-02) components are mounted.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05) and observe QrDisplayPanel.
Verify section code and session name appear in panel header alongside Open StatusBadge.
Assert QR canvas is high-contrast black-on-white without radius, gradients, or shadow on QR modules per DC-01 spec.
Verify QR occupies substantial viewport area (≥ 60% height in projection mode) and QrCountdownRing shows numeric seconds-remaining.
Confirm SessionControlBar shows room and scheduled time context per wireframe PG-05.
cases.expected: Browser journey satisfies AC-UI-06 projection legibility: large high-contrast QR suitable for classroom projector with session identity, Open status badge, and countdown refresh indicator visible on one screen.

cases.title: QrDisplayPanel auto-refreshes QR before TTL expiry without manual page reload
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
An Open session is displayed on PG-05 with QrDisplayPanel and QrCountdownRing visible.
Session name, section code, and Open status badge are rendered.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05) and observe QrDisplayPanel with high-contrast QR image and countdown indicator.
Note the visible countdown value and QR image fingerprint or payload preview.
Wait past one 30-second TTL cycle without refreshing the browser tab.
Observe QrCountdownRing reset and QR image crossfade or swap to a new token before prior expiry.
Verify session badge remains Open, session name and section code stay visible, and no full-page reload occurred.
Confirm refreshed QR remains scannable for student check-in flow.
cases.expected: Browser journey demonstrates FR-14 projection-friendly display: QR refreshes automatically before 30-second TTL expires via QrDisplayPanel/QrCountdownRing without lecturer manual reload, maintaining Open-state indicators throughout rotation.

cases.title: Closed session PG-05 hides QR canvas and shows locked state message with Closed badge
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
A Closed session fixture exists with prior attendance data.
Session was previously Open with active QR display.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{closedSessionId} (PG-05).
Verify StatusBadge shows Closed state.
Assert QrDisplayPanel does not render scannable QR canvas; locked state message is visible instead.
Confirm Open/Close attendance CTAs are disabled or hidden per wireframe Closed state.
Verify section code and session name remain visible for classroom context.
cases.expected: PG-05 Closed state per wireframe §4.2: QR locked message replaces projection canvas; Closed badge displayed; no active countdown or rotating QR; session identity remains legible.

cases.title: Concurrent qr/current fetches during display rotation return consistent projection metadata
preconditions: Open session exists with QR rotation scheduler active.
Integration harness can dispatch parallel GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current requests.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is established.
steps: Fire multiple parallel GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current requests at a fixed instant before TTL boundary.
Collect all responses and assert each returns HTTP 200 with tokenState Valid for the same sessionId.
Assert all responses share the same qrPayload and expiresAt for the current rotation window.
Repeat parallel fetches immediately after TTL rollover and confirm all responses converge on the next valid token without corrupt display state.
cases.expected: Concurrent reads during a rotation window return consistent current token metadata for projection display; post-TTL parallel reads converge on the next Valid token without duplicate-active Valid rows violating display invariants.
